record.field: Matomo is a self-hosted, privacy-first web analytics platform that measures visits, campaigns and conversions while keeping 100% of your data on your own servers.

Matomo is a self-hosted, privacy-first web analytics platform that measures visits, campaigns and conversions while keeping 100% of your data on your own servers.

Matomo (formerly Piwik) is the leading open-source alternative to Google Analytics: it tracks visitors, page views, campaigns, goals and e-commerce funnels through a rich web dashboard, but stores every raw hit inside your own database. Because the data never leaves your infrastructure, you own it outright, can honour GDPR/ePrivacy without sharing visitor behaviour with third parties, and can run cookieless, IP-anonymised measurement. It suits marketing teams, agencies and any organisation that wants trustworthy analytics without surrendering user data.

Key features
  • Full web analytics: visits, page views, referrers, campaigns, goals and events
  • E-commerce tracking with revenue, orders, abandoned carts and product reports
  • Privacy tools: IP anonymisation, cookieless tracking, data retention and opt-out
  • Heatmaps, session recordings, funnels and A/B testing via optional plugins
  • 100% data ownership with unlimited websites, users and a full reporting API
  • Real-time visitor log, custom dashboards, segments and scheduled email reports
Best for

Marketing teams, agencies and privacy-conscious organisations that need accurate, GDPR-friendly analytics they fully control. Put it behind HTTPS; after the first-run installer, add your public domain to Matomo's trusted hosts and enable proxy header handling so reverse-proxy traffic is measured correctly.

services: db: image: mariadb:11.4 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_db' environment: MARIADB_DATABASE: {{ db_name }} MARIADB_USER: {{ db_user }} MARIADB_PASSWORD: {{ db_password }} MARIADB_ROOT_PASSWORD: {{ mariadb_root_password }} volumes: - ./db-data:/var/lib/mysql expose: - 3306 healthcheck: test: ["CMD-SHELL", "healthcheck.sh --connect --innodb_initialized"] interval: 10s timeout: 5s retries: 5 start_period: 30s restart: unless-stopped networks: - internal matomo: image: matomo:latest container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' depends_on: db: condition: service_healthy environment: MATOMO_DATABASE_HOST: db MATOMO_DATABASE_ADAPTER: mysql MATOMO_DATABASE_TABLES_PREFIX: {{ db_table_prefix }} MATOMO_DATABASE_USERNAME: {{ db_user }} MATOMO_DATABASE_PASSWORD: {{ db_password }} MATOMO_DATABASE_DBNAME: {{ db_name }} PHP_MEMORY_LIMIT: {{ php_memory_limit }} volumes: - ./matomo-data:/var/www/html ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:80' restart: unless-stopped networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump de la base de APLICACIÓN de Matomo. Clave: usa MYSQL_PWD (env), NUNCA `-p` — con # `-p` y password vacío mysql/mariadb espera un prompt por stdin y CUELGA. Prefiere el # usuario de app (MARIADB_USER/PASSWORD/DATABASE); cae a root solo si no hay usuario de app # (excluye schemas de sistema, que romperían la auth al restaurar). docker compose exec -T db sh -c ' # MariaDB 11+ renombró mysqldump→mariadb-dump y mysql→mariadb: detectamos el binario. DUMP=$(command -v mysqldump || command -v mariadb-dump); CLI=$(command -v mysql || command -v mariadb) if [ -n "${MARIADB_USER:-}" ] && [ -n "${MARIADB_PASSWORD:-}" ] && [ -n "${MARIADB_DATABASE:-}" ]; then export MYSQL_PWD="$MARIADB_PASSWORD" exec "$DUMP" -u"$MARIADB_USER" --single-transaction --databases "$MARIADB_DATABASE" fi export MYSQL_PWD="${MARIADB_ROOT_PASSWORD:-}" DBS=$("$CLI" -uroot -N -e "SHOW DATABASES" | grep -Ev "^(mysql|sys|information_schema|performance_schema)$") [ -z "$DBS" ] && { echo "REVISAR: sin bases de aplicacion para respaldar" >&2; exit 0; } exec "$DUMP" -uroot --single-transaction --databases $DBS ' > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.sql" echo "mysqldump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.sql") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" docker compose up -d db # Readiness + restore en UN exec (credenciales DENTRO del contenedor, con MYSQL_PWD → # jamás cuelga esperando un prompt de password). Prefiere el usuario de app; cae a root. docker compose exec -T db sh -c ' CLI=$(command -v mysql || command -v mariadb) if [ -n "${MARIADB_USER:-}" ] && [ -n "${MARIADB_PASSWORD:-}" ]; then U="$MARIADB_USER"; export MYSQL_PWD="$MARIADB_PASSWORD"; else U=root; export MYSQL_PWD="${MARIADB_ROOT_PASSWORD:-}"; fi for i in $(seq 1 60); do "$CLI" -u"$U" -e "SELECT 1" >/dev/null 2>&1 && break; sleep 2; done exec "$CLI" -u"$U" ' < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.sql" echo "restore MySQL OK"
Root password for the MariaDB database server.
Password for the Matomo application database user.
Prefix applied to all Matomo tables.
PHP memory_limit for the Matomo container (Matomo recommends at least 128M).
